tweet: The mystery:  Why won’t @FarmBureau and @BeefUSA work with us on the text of this legislation for the benefit of their members?  Are you a member of one of these organizations? Please ask them why they don’t want to be part of this legislation to help small farmers.

name: Thomas Massie
tweet: The solution:  Our bipartisan, bicameral PRIME Act (which we are about to reintroduce) would allow small processors who comply with state regulations to sell within their states without being overly regulated by USDA.

name: Thomas Massie
tweet: The consequences:  Farmers are unable to get a fair price for their livestock, while the price of meat increases in grocery stores. Due to the centralized nature of the processing, shortages sometimes occur, and when outbreaks of food borne illness occur, it affects many states.

name: Thomas Massie
tweet: The problem:  Four corporations control 85% of meat processed in the United States. One of these companies is Chinese, one is Brazilian. Prices are going up in the supermarkets. The oligopoly is enabled by over-regulation of small American owned processors by the US government.

name: Thomas Massie
tweet: @eliyak That article disingenuously employs the word “extend.” Their sentence gives a glimpse of the truth though: “generic manufacturers have less incentive to make a version of insulin that doctors perceived as obsolete.” Patents aren’t keeping the original insulin from going generic.

name: Thomas Massie
tweet: @MAGADAY1 I think virtually all inventions and discoveries should be patentable, regardless of the medium or domain.

name: Thomas Massie
tweet: @eliyak “extending patents” is a myth.  They can’t be extended beyond their term. Only the improvements can be covered by a new patent, while everything disclosed in the original patent becomes public domain.

name: Thomas Massie
tweet: @JerseyGirlGinny The closest analogs to patents are deeds. Keeping the official registry of “who owns what” so conflicts (theft and trespass) can be resolved in court of law is a universally recognized role of government.
